message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="heydon-pickering" color="red" summary="CONSENSUS APPROVED for Step 7 — no blocking items">
**Step 7 (Clarify as Project) -- Accessibility Review**

I have reviewed the template, CSS, route handler, and integration tests.

**No BLOCKING items.**

This implementation follows all of the accessibility guidance I provided to Carson during the design discussion. Here is what I verified:

**Correct patterns applied:**

1. **Fieldset/Legend** (`inbox_item.html` lines 12-13): `<fieldset>` wraps the radio group and related fields. `<legend class="visually-hidden">Clarify: {{ item.title }}</legend>` gives screen reader users full context about what decision they are making. Correct.

2. **Radio buttons with visible labels** (lines 15-22): Wrapping `<label>` elements make the text a click/tap target and provide accessible names. `name="clarify_type"` groups them per form. "Next Action" is `checked` by default, which is the right default for the common case. Correct.

3. **Progressive disclosure via CSS `:has()`** (CSS line 1267-1276): Project fields use `display: none` by default and `display: flex` when `input[value="project"]:checked`. This correctly removes the fields from the accessibility tree when not relevant -- a screen reader in "Next Action" mode will not encounter the "First action" input. Correct.

4. **Visible label on first action input** (lines 33-34): `<label for="first-action-{{ item.id }}">First action</label>` with unique ID per inbox item. Correct.

5. **Visible label on context select** (lines 25-26): Carried forward from the existing pattern. `<label for="clarify-context-{{ item.id }}">Context</label>` with unique ID. Correct.

6. **Accessible name on submit button** (line 44): `aria-label="Clarify: {{ item.title }}"` -- distinguishes each button when multiple inbox items are on-screen. Correct.

7. **Trash remains a separate form** (lines 47-56): Destructive action is not folded into the radio group. Correct.

8. **No HTML `required` on first action input**: The input has no `required` attribute, and validation is server-side (`TodoTitle::parse` in the service, returning 422 on empty). This prevents the browser from blocking submission of a "Next Action" clarify due to a hidden required field. Correct.

9. **HTMX announce pattern** (route handler line 239): `htmx_response_with_announce("Clarified as project")` populates the live region. Correct.

10. **Focus management**: The existing `inbox-focus.js` already handles item removal after HTMX swap (outerHTML with empty body). The clarify-as-project response follows the same pattern (empty body, item removed), so focus management works without changes. Correct.

**Minor (non-blocking):**

- M1: The submit button text is a static "Clarify" regardless of which radio is selected. A screen reader user must remember which radio they checked before pressing "Clarify". A small JS progressive enhancement could make the button text dynamic ("Next Action" / "Create Project"), but the current `aria-label="Clarify: {{ item.title }}"` combined with the `<legend>` context is sufficient for WCAG compliance. Nice-to-have for a future polish pass.

**Integration tests** (lines 807-833): Tests verify the `aria-label` on both Clarify and Trash buttons, the presence of radio options, and the `first_action_title` input. Good coverage.

**CONSENSUS APPROVED** from Heydon Pickering (Accessibility).
</teammate-message>

content.text:
Heydon's Step 7 accessibility review is in -- APPROVED with no blocking issues. Thorough review confirming all 10 accessibility patterns are correct.

Updated tally:
- 8 APPROVED with detailed reviews: Luca, Kent, Scott, Marty, Heydon, Lea, Steve Schoger, Carson (Driver)
- 1 BLOCKING: Steve Krug -- validation error dead-end page

Still waiting for Carson to fix Steve Krug's blocking item. That's the only thing standing between us and 9/9 consensus.
